flying_page.title: Flying Bird
flying_page.subtitle: A 3D remake of Flappy Bird focused on C++ gameplay architecture and cross-platform deployment.

about.title: Technical Recreation
about.desc: Flying Bird is a personal study project designed to practice the Unreal Engine mobile pipeline. The objective was to recreate classic mechanics using a full C++ backend.

gameplay.title: Core Logic
items: • Velocity Reset: Custom flight logic that normalizes vertical speed before each impulse for consistent jumps.
• Procedural Loop: Spawner system managing the lifecycle and randomized Z-axis offsetting of obstacles.

tech.title: C++ Architecture
items: • Event-Driven: Usage of Dynamic Multicast Delegates to decouple gameplay logic from UI and Audio.
• Data Persistence: Full C++ implementation of SaveGame for local high scores and settings.

systems.title: Mobile Optimization
items: • Cross-Platform: Use of #if PLATFORM_ANDROID macros for dynamic UI injection.
• Code-Driven UI: Widget hierarchy parented to C++ classes with programmatic Slate styling.

game_state.title: Event Architecture
game_state.desc: Usage of Dynamic Multicast Delegates in the GameState to decouple gameplay triggers (Score, Death) from the UI and audio systems.
game_state.img1: Delegate System: Implementation of event broadcasting to maintain a clean communication flow between classes.

bird.title: Physics & Impulse
bird.desc: Flight logic handled via AddImpulse. The system dampens angular velocity and locks rotation to maintain stability during physics simulation.
bird.img1: Impulse Logic: A view of the impulse force parameters and the air resistance (Linear Damping) setup.

pipes.title: Collision Management
pipes.desc: Optimization of collision channels to separate lethal meshes from scoring triggers, preventing false-positive overlaps.
pipes.img1: Collision Filtering: Configuration of custom object types for accurate interaction detection.

spawner.title: Procedural Spawning
spawner.desc: Implementation of a tick-based generator that uses FFloatRange to randomize obstacle height within set bounds.
spawner.img1: Spawner Setup: View of the spawner properties, including spawn delays and vertical range offsets.

input.title: Cross-Platform Input
input.desc: Unified mapping via Enhanced Input, allowing the same logic to handle Android touch events and Windows keyboard/mouse clicks.
input.img1: IMC_Input: Configuration showing the mapping for spacebar and screen taps.

ui.title: Android Optimization
ui.desc: Usage of C++ preprocessor macros (#if PLATFORM_ANDROID) to dynamically inject mobile-specific UI elements like the pause button.
ui.img1: Platform HUD: Conditional logic for mobile-only elements.
ui.img2: Slate Styling: Programmatic button styling in C++ to handle Normal/Pressed states without Blueprint overhead.
ui.img3: C++ Parenting: Demonstration of Widget Blueprints inheriting from C++ classes for optimized logic.

cta.title: Ready for a challenge?
cta.subtitle: Test the physics and try to beat your high score. Available for Android and Windows.
